title: The One With The Vows

jokes.text: Do you realize you get louder each week?
jokes.setup: Chandler's excited exclamation about wedding timing
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact observation about his escalating volume
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven humor that plays into Monica's practical nature contrasted with Chandler's escalating wedding anxiety. Well-structured with good economy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're getting married!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes, we are.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: WE'RE GETTING MARRIED!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And apparently the further away the wedding is, the louder you have to yell about it.

jokes.text: I figured I'd just buy those. "Pat, I'd like to buy a vow."
jokes.setup: Monica asking if he's written his wedding vows
jokes.punchline: Chandler treating vows like a Wheel of Fortune purchase
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that perfectly captures Chandler's avoidance of emotional sincerity through pop culture deflection. The Wheel of Fortune reference is specific and well-executed.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Have you written your vows yet?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'd like to buy a vowel.

jokes.text: You know I've no sense of humor when it comes to the wedding.
jokes.setup: Chandler just made a joke about buying vows
jokes.punchline: Monica's deadpan declaration about having no humor regarding weddings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character moment - her control-freak nature about the wedding delivered with characteristic bluntness.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I have no sense of humor about my wedding.

jokes.text: Look at her go. She must love me more than I love her!
jokes.setup: Monica writing her vows enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Chandler's competitive/insecure interpretation of vow-writing speed
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Chandler's neurotic tendency to turn everything into competition or self-doubt.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm writing my vows and I can't stop! I've already filled three pages!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Three pages? Monica, vows aren't a hostage negotiation. How long are you planning to make yours?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I don't know, as long as it takes to express how I feel.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I'm on page four. So I win.

jokes.text: What's wrong with me? Don't open that door.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking what's wrong with him
jokes.punchline: Immediately warning himself not to explore that question
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and self-awareness. Classic Chandler neurotic humor with perfect timing and character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What's wrong with me?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no, no. Don't go there.

jokes.text: There are no words! This shouldn't be this hard!
jokes.setup: Starting with 'Monica, there are no words...'
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself by saying there literally are no words
jokes.explanation: Solid structural irony showing Chandler's frustration, though somewhat predictable for the character.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, there are no words...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, actually, there are words. A lot of words. But none of them are appropriate.

jokes.text: With Carol, I promised never to love another woman until the day I die. She made no such promise.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking what Ross said in his previous wedding vows
jokes.punchline: The darkly ironic revelation about Carol's lack of reciprocal commitment
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy that perfectly encapsulates Ross's divorce situation. Great economy and bitter irony delivered with perfect deadpan timing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What did you say in your vows?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I promised to love her forever, to be faithful, to support her dreams...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And what did Carol say?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: She said 'I do' and then six months later 'I'm leaving you for a woman.'

jokes.text: Except your breasts look kind of small. Those are my eyes! Those are my breasts.
jokes.setup: Phoebe commenting on Monica's wedding dress drawing
jokes.punchline: The confusion about body parts in the crude drawing
jokes.explanation: Solid visual comedy setup with Phoebe's characteristic bluntness, though the anatomical confusion is fairly predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Is that the dress? Because it looks like you drew a uterus.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That is not a uterus!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, okay. Well, then where's the neck?

jokes.text: All right, there's a nuclear holocaust. I'm the last man on earth. Would you go out with me?
jokes.setup: Flashback to Monica and Chandler's first meeting and Ross introducing them
jokes.punchline: Chandler's hilariously desperate hypothetical pickup line
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler self-deprecating humor with excellent escalation from normal pickup to apocalyptic desperation. Very quotable and character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Monica, this is my friend Chandler. Chandler, this is my sister Monica.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hi Monica. So, uh, do you believe in love at first sight, or should I walk by again?

jokes.text: There are seven? That's one? I was looking at it upside down.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining erogenous zones in flashback
jokes.punchline: Chandler's confusion about anatomy and perspective
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy playing on sexual education humor, though fairly straightforward in execution.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So there are all these erogenous zones on a woman's body.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. You're telling me there are erogenous zones I don't know about?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes! Like the small of the back, behind the ears, the inside of the wrist...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The inside of the wrist? What am I supposed to do, lick it like a stamp?

jokes.text: Well, you know, sometimes that helps.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying he was looking at the diagram upside down
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact sexual advice
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery from Monica with perfect sexual innuendo that feels natural to her character's directness.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, there's your problem.

jokes.text: ...a four, a three-two... two, a two-four-six. Two-four-six, four, two... two, four-seven... five-seven, six-seven... seven, seven... seven, seven, seven, seven, seven... seven!
jokes.setup: Monica demonstrating erogenous zone technique
jokes.punchline: The escalating sequence culminating in repetitive 'sevens'
jokes.explanation: Iconic Friends moment with perfect escalation and rhythm. The mathematical absurdity combined with obvious sexual climax metaphor is brilliantly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, so you start here, very lightly...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Then you move to here, a little bit harder...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And then you can really go to town right... here.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Seven! Seven! Seven!

jokes.text: No fair! I don't even have one, how come they get two?
jokes.setup: Seeing twins in the park
jokes.punchline: Monica treating babies like unfair resource distribution
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Monica's competitive nature applied to motherhood in an absurd way.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, twins! Look at them, they're so perfect. One for me, one for Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, you can't just claim babies in the park.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why not? Nobody else is doing anything with them right now.

jokes.text: Why wouldn't I be married when I'm 40? Hypothetically, why won't I be married when I'm 40? What is it? Seriously, is there something fundamentally un-marriable about me?
jokes.setup: Chandler's innocent hypothetical about having babies at 40
jokes.punchline: Monica's spiraling insecurity about her marriageability
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Monica's neuroses about marriage and relationships. The repetitive questioning builds genuine comedic tension.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Are you saying that you don't want to have babies with me?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no, no! I'm just saying hypothetically, if we were both still single at 40—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Oh my God! So you DO think about us not being together! What is it? Is it my hair? Do you not find me attractive anymore?

jokes.text: Dear God, this parachute is a knapsack!
jokes.setup: Monica asking if there's something un-marriable about her
jokes.punchline: Chandler's complete subject change to imagined skydiving disaster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant non-sequitur showing Chandler's panic avoidance. The specificity of the parachute/knapsack confusion is absurdly perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Is there something un-marriable about me?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? No! Of course not. You're very marriable. I mean, if I wasn't... you know, married to you, I would totally want to marry you.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Although, I gotta say, skydiving without a parachute? Now that's un-marriable.

jokes.text: Nothing! I said, you're so great, and then I just stopped talking.
jokes.setup: Monica asking what he said after saying 'I love you'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's obvious lie about what he actually said
jokes.explanation: Solid awkward comedy showing Chandler's fear of emotional commitment, though the situation is fairly predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What did you say?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I said... 'I love you too'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No you didn't!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I said it in my head!

jokes.text: You said you love me! I can't believe this. - No, I didn't. - Yes, you did. - No, I didn't! - You love me! - No, I don't!
jokes.setup: Monica confronting Chandler about saying 'I love you'
jokes.punchline: The childish back-and-forth denial escalating to physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Basic escalation comedy that relies more on physical elements than clever writing. Functional but not particularly original.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Did you or did you not say 'I love you'?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't know what you're talking about.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I didn't say it.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You totally said it!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Nuh-uh.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yuh-huh!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Nuh-uh!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yuh-huh!

jokes.text: What if we live together, and you understand what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Chandler's roundabout way of suggesting moving in together
jokes.punchline: His inability to directly state what he wants
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's inability to be direct about emotional topics, though the setup is somewhat drawn out.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So, hypothetically, if two people were living in the same apartment, and they really liked each other, that would be... okay, right? I mean, not that I'm suggesting anything. I'm just saying, in theory, if someone wanted to ask someone else if they wanted to, you know, cohabitate in a non-roommate way, how would that person go about doing that without completely freaking out the other person?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, are you asking me to move in with you?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I was asking in a very roundabout, complicated way that made it seem like I didn't know what I was talking about. If you'd just let me continue, I could have confused you for another twenty minutes or so.

jokes.text: There's a reason why girls don't do this!
jokes.setup: Monica struggling with proposing to Chandler
jokes.punchline: Her practical observation about gender roles in proposals
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor that feels natural for Monica's practical character, though the observation itself is fairly obvious.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I want to propose to Chandler, but what if he thinks it's weird that I'm asking him? What if he wants to be the one to propose?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I mean, it's 2003, but apparently some traditions die hard.

jokes.text: How is "Monica, I love your sweet ass" inappropriate?
jokes.setup: Explanation that Joey made inappropriate vow suggestions
jokes.punchline: Joey's genuine confusion about what makes this inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment showing his complete lack of filter and genuine confusion about social appropriateness. Very quotable and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? I don't get it. What's wrong with that?

jokes.text: Let's say it's lucky she's got a sweet ass because she's not so good at the writing.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking how Monica's doing with vow writing
jokes.punchline: Ross incorporating Joey's inappropriate language into his assessment
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows the inappropriate language spreading, with Ross trying to be practical while using Joey's crude terminology.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: How's Monica doing with her vows?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, she's doing great. Joey's been helping her out.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That's nice.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, she's got some really beautiful stuff written down, but apparently Joey's been giving her some suggestions too. She asked me what I thought and I told her, 'Monica, that's beautiful, but maybe lose the part where you talk about how you're gonna bang Chandler like a screen door in a hurricane.'

jokes.text: Nine out of 10 marriages end in divorce. - Phoebe, that's not true. - Yeah, you're right. How's the missus?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Monica and Chandler's upcoming marriage
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual transition to asking about Ross's own failed marriage
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark comedy with perfect Phoebe obliviousness. The transition from statistics to personal reality is well-executed.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So Monica and Chandler are getting married! That's so exciting!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know, right? I'm really happy for them.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah... So when are you getting married again?

jokes.text: Chicken? I could eat some chicken. Hey, Joe!
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining someone kept asking about chicken
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately wanting chicken and calling for 'Joe'
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation humor with Ross getting caught up in the chicken mention, though not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Someone kept asking about chicken.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Chicken? Joe! JOE!

jokes.text: Laundry? Is that my new nickname?
jokes.setup: Chandler saying he'll tell Rachel he's doing laundry
jokes.punchline: Monica interpreting 'laundry' as a sexual nickname
jokes.explanation: Decent sexual innuendo that plays on the secretive nature of their relationship, though not particularly sophisticated.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'll just tell Rachel I'm doing laundry.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Doing laundry? That's what you're calling it?

jokes.text: You know what your nickname is, Mr. Big...
jokes.setup: Monica responding about nicknames
jokes.punchline: The obvious sexual reference with 'Mr. Big'
jokes.explanation: Basic sexual innuendo that relies on obvious implications rather than clever wordplay.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Mr. Big? That's not a nickname, that's a sex thing!

jokes.text: It's okay! It's okay! - No! They're doing it! I know! I know! - You know? - Yes, I know!
jokes.setup: Phoebe walking in on Monica and Chandler
jokes.punchline: The escalating revelation of who knows about the relationship
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation comedy building the web of secrets, though the execution is somewhat chaotic.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I knew it! I knew it!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, what are you—

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You and Chandler! Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, so you know.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: How long has this been going on?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A few months, we were going to tell everyone—

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, everyone knows! Rachel knows, Joey knows, Ross knows, even Ben knows!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Ben's seven!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, and he figured it out before you two told anyone.

jokes.text: Or we could not tell them we know and have a little fun of our own.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to reveal they know about Monica and Chandler
jokes.punchline: Rachel's mischievous counter-proposal
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the elaborate deception storyline that follows. Sets up multiple layers of knowledge and deception.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, you want to tell them we know? Okay, fine. But then I get to tell Monica that I saw her trying on wedding dresses, and I get to tell Chandler that he cried during 'The Notebook,' and I get to tell both of them that sometimes at night I can hear them through the wall, and it sounds like—

jokes.text: The messers become the messees!
jokes.setup: Learning that others know about them and are 'messing' with them
jokes.punchline: Monica's made-up word for role reversal
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that feels natural for Monica's competitive nature. The made-up 'messees' is memorable and character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, you guys know? And you're messing with us?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's... that's role reversal! I'm being Chandler!

jokes.text: They don't know that we know they know we know.
jokes.setup: The complex web of who knows what
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's tongue-twisting summary of the situation
jokes.explanation: Iconic Friends line that perfectly captures the absurd complexity of the situation. Brilliant verbal comedy with perfect rhythm and character specificity.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, so Monica and Chandler are together, and Ross doesn't know that Rachel knows that Joey knows that Monica told Phoebe that Chandler... wait, no. Rachel told Joey that Monica told Chandler that Ross knows that... um... okay, I have no idea what's going on anymore.

jokes.text: Couldn't if I wanted to.
jokes.setup: Being told he can't say anything about the situation
jokes.punchline: Joey's honest admission that he's too confused to explain anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment showing his confusion about complex situations. Great economy and character truth.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can't say anything about this.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Besides, I have no idea what's going on anyway.

jokes.text: Well, come here. I'm very happy we're gonna have all the sex.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to seduce Chandler as part of the game
jokes.punchline: Her awkward and clinical approach to seduction
jokes.explanation: Hilarious character comedy showing Phoebe's unique approach to fake seduction. The clinical 'all the sex' is perfectly awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Hey Chandler, I'm gonna seduce you now. So you should probably get ready for that.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay...

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm gonna start by batting my eyelashes. This is me batting my eyelashes.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I can see that.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Now I'm gonna touch your arm. Okay, I'm touching your arm. How's that?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's... it's good.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Now I'm gonna lean in and whisper something sexy in your ear. The thing I'm about to whisper is... 'I want you.' Okay, here I go.

jokes.text: You should be. I'm very bendy.
jokes.setup: Continuing the fake seduction
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact sexual boast
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe line that's both sexual and innocent. The straightforward delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm very bendy.

jokes.text: Okay, okay, okay, fine, you win!
jokes.setup: The seduction game escalating to actual kissing
jokes.punchline: Phoebe breaking first and admitting defeat
jokes.explanation: Solid payoff to the escalating seduction game, though the resolution is fairly predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, I can't do this anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You win. I'm not strong enough.

jokes.text: I love her! That's right, I love her! I love her!
jokes.setup: Chandler finally breaking under pressure
jokes.punchline: His repeated, desperate declarations of love
jokes.explanation: Good emotional release after the buildup. Shows Chandler finally being honest about his feelings under extreme circumstances.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I love you. I love you. I love you!

jokes.text: I thought you guys were doing it. I didn't know you were in love.
jokes.setup: The revelation that Monica and Chandler are in love
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual distinction between sex and love
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe line that shows her casual attitude toward sex vs. the significance of love. Great character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Monica and Chandler? But they're so... Monica and Chandler. Wait, have they... you know?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, they slept together.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, that's not love, that's just sex.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, Pheebs, they're in love.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! Well, why didn't you say so?

jokes.text: What are you doing? Get off my sister!
jokes.setup: Ross walking in on Monica and Chandler kissing
jokes.punchline: His protective brother reaction
jokes.explanation: Predictable but well-executed physical comedy. Ross's protective reaction is character-appropriate but not particularly original.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! What is going on here?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ross, we can explain...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Chandler?! My best friend? My sister?!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We were just—

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't want to hear it! Monica, you're my little sister. And Chandler, you're like a brother to me. This is... this is weird!

jokes.text: But I don't want them to move to a Volvo dealership.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Monica and Chandler moving near a Volvo dealership
jokes.punchline: Joey's confusion about moving near vs. moving to the dealership
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey confusion that's both endearing and genuinely funny. Shows his literal interpretation of language.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. You're moving near a Volvo dealership? Why would you move near a Volvo dealership?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We're not moving TO the dealership, Joey. We're moving to a house that happens to be near one.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh. Oh! I thought you guys were like, moving into the showroom or something.

jokes.text: That little naked guy would be me. Yes, yes, fine, that is my penis. Can we be grownups now?
jokes.setup: Someone pointing out a naked person in old photos
jokes.punchline: Ross's mortified admission about childhood nudity
jokes.explanation: Solid embarrassment humor that plays on childhood photo awkwardness. Ross's exasperation feels genuine.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: In my defense, I was going through a very experimental phase.

jokes.text: Let me see. No, that would be me again.
jokes.setup: More photo examination
jokes.punchline: Another instance of Ross being naked in photos
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to the previous naked photo joke. Effective but not particularly creative.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Why do I keep ending up naked in these photos?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because you have no sense of self-awareness?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's not helpful, Chandler.

jokes.text: Don't worry, honey. We'll make yours funnier.
jokes.setup: Chandler's vows getting laughs when they should be touching
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical solution to fix the tone mismatch
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's practical approach to problem-solving, even in emotional moments.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Maybe you should just skip the vows and go straight to the kiss.

jokes.text: How did you write this? I stole Monica's and changed the name.
jokes.setup: Joey asking how Chandler wrote such good vows
jokes.punchline: Chandler's admission that he plagiarized Monica's vows
jokes.explanation: Excellent punchline that perfectly caps the vow-writing struggle storyline. Classic Chandler solution that's both lazy and clever.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, how did you write those vows? They were incredible!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, actually... I didn't write them. I kind of... borrowed them from Monica's vows.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You plagiarized your wife's vows?!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: In my defense, they were really good, and I panicked!

jokes.text: If he goes first, he can!
jokes.setup: Objection that Chandler can't steal Monica's vows
jokes.punchline: Joey's practical solution based on presentation order
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic that's both helpful and morally questionable. Shows his practical problem-solving approach.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! Why can't he just switch a couple words around? You know, like, 'I, Chandler, take you, Monica' instead of 'I, Monica, take you, Chandler.' Problem solved!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? I'm just saying, nobody's gonna notice if you read 'em in a different order!

tmdb_overview: Monica and Chandler are getting married in four weeks and have to write their vows, but neither has a clue what to say, so they reflect on their past. In the end, the only real problem is who will be saying the vows first.
insight: Chandler's panic escalates relentlessly through 42 jokes—wedding-day chaos peaks at 2.66 gags per minute.
